heading: Civil money penalty
label: 504 Civil money penalty
notes: CodificationIn subsec. (a), “section 371c, 371c–1, 375, 375a, 375b, 376, or 503 of this title” was in the original “section 22, 23A, or 23B”, meaning section 22, 23A, or 23B of the Federal Reserve Act. Sections 23A and 23B are classified to sections 371c and 371c–1, respectively, of this title. Subsections (d) to (h) of section 22 are classified to sections 375, 375a, 375b, 376, and 503 of this title. The text of was struck out by , , .section 375 of this titlePub. L. 111–203, title VI, § 615(b)124 Stat. 1615July 21, 2010

(a), making loans, extensions of credit, purchases of securities, etc., respecting affiliates, executive officers, etc.; subsec. (b), amount of penalty; subsec. (c), opportunity for hearing; subsec. (d), review by United States court of appeals; subsec. (e), action by Attorney General for failure to pay assessment; subsec. (f), promulgation of regulations; and subsec. (g), penalties covered into Treasury of United States.Pub. L. 101–73, § 907(g)Subsec. (m). , added subsec. (m).Pub. L. 101–73, § 905(f)1982—Subsec. (a). , (d)(1), inserted proviso giving agency discretionary authority to compromise, etc., any civil money penalty imposed under its authority, and substituted “may be assessed” for “shall be assessed”, respectively.Pub. children.heading: First tier
children.label: a First tier
children.text: Any member bank which, and any institution-affiliated party (within the meaning of ) with respect to such member bank who, violates any provision of section 371c, 371c–1, 375, 375a, 375b, 376, or 503 of this title, or any regulation issued pursuant thereto, shall forfeit and pay a civil penalty of not more than $5,000 for each day during which such violation continues.section 1813(u) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: commits any violation described in subsection (a);

children.label: B
children.text: recklessly engages in an unsafe or unsound practice in conducting the affairs of such member bank; or

children.label: C
children.text: breaches any fiduciary duty;

children.label: A
children.text: is part of a pattern of misconduct;

children.label: B
children.text: causes or is likely to cause more than a minimal loss to such member bank; or

children.label: C
children.text: results in pecuniary gain or other benefit to such party,

children.label: A
children.text: commits any violation described in subsection (a);

children.label: B
children.text: engages in any unsafe or unsound practice in conducting the affairs of such credit union; or2So in original. Probably should be “such member bank”.2

children.label: C
children.text: breaches any fiduciary duty; and
knowingly or recklessly causes a substantial loss to such credit union or a substantial pecuniary gain or other benefit to such party by reason of such violation, practice, or breach,2

children.heading: Maximum amounts of penalties for any violation described in subsection (c)
children.label: d Maximum amounts of penalties for any violation described in subsection (c)

children.text: in the case of any person other than a member bank, an amount to not exceed $1,000,000; and

children.label: B
children.text: 1 percent of the total assets of such member bank.
in the case of a national bank, by the Comptroller of the Currency; and
in the case of a State member bank, by the Board,

children.heading: Hearing
children.label: f Hearing
children.text: The member bank or other person against whom any penalty is assessed under this section shall be afforded an agency hearing if such member bank or person submits a request for such hearing within 20 days after the issuance of the notice of assessment. shall apply to any proceeding under this section.Section 1818(h) of this title

children.heading: Disbursement
children.label: g Disbursement
children.text: All penalties collected under authority of this paragraph shall be deposited into the Treasury.

children.heading: “Violate” defined
children.label: h “Violate” defined
children.text: For purposes of this section, the term “violate” includes any action (alone or with another or others) for or toward causing, bringing about, participating in, counseling, or aiding or abetting a violation.

children.heading: Regulations
children.label: i Regulations
children.text: The Comptroller of the Currency and the Board shall prescribe regulations establishing such procedures as may be necessary to carry out this section.

children.heading: Notice under this section after separation from service4So in original. No subsecs. (j) to () have been enacted.4l
children.label: m Notice under this section after separation from service4So in original. No subsecs. (j) to () have been enacted.4l
children.text: The resignation, termination of employment or participation, or separation of an institution-affiliated party (within the meaning of ) with respect to a member bank (including a separation caused by the closing of such a bank) shall not affect the jurisdiction and authority of the appropriate Federal banking agency to issue any notice and proceed under this section against any such party, if such notice is served before the end of the 6-year period beginning on the date such party ceased to be such a party with respect to such bank (whether such date occurs before, on, or after ).section 1813(u) of this titleAugust 9, 1989
